query: I want to add `NOT NULL` to the existing `signup_source` column on a 1.2 million row table. An unknown fraction of rows have NULL values. What's the safe migration?
expected_behavior: Classifies adding NOT NULL to an existing nullable column with existing NULLs as a BREAKING change and refuses to emit a single-statement `ALTER COLUMN ... SET NOT NULL` without first handling the existing NULLs
Recommends backfilling the existing NULL rows with a sentinel / default value first (e.g., 'unknown' or '__legacy__'), preferably in batches to avoid long-running locks
Recommends adding a column DEFAULT before (or together with) the NOT NULL constraint so future INSERTs that omit the column also succeed
